item.title: Mask Network's April Updates Highlight Web3 Momentum | Mask Network spotlights key developments in decentralized social and AI-driven Web3 infra... https://Blockchain.News/news/mask-network-web3-updates-april-2026
item.description:
Original title: Mask Network's April Updates Highlight Web3 Momentum Summary: Mask Network spotlights key developments in decentralized social and AI-driven Web3 infrastructure during April 2026. Article text: Mask Network spotlights key developments in decentralized social and AI-driven Web3 infrastructure during April 2026.
April 2026 saw Mask Network doubling down on decentralized social infrastructure, even as the broader Web3 market oscillated between hype over AI speculation and tangible builder progress. With Bitcoin (BTC) climbing past $78,000—a sign of renewed optimism—the focus on meaningful development couldn’t be more timely.
Mask Network’s updates showcased its commitment to advancing digital sovereignty in an era increasingly shaped by AI dominance. The team emphasized that decentralized social isn’t just a feature—it’s a foundational layer for reclaiming user control. April’s highlights included:
Building on its first season’s success, Firefly launched Season 2 of its Polymarket Trading League, introducing a 3,000 USDC prize pool, daily trading rewards, and leaderboard incentives. By integrating social sharing features, Firefly continues to blend trading with community engagement, enhancing its appeal in the decentralized finance ecosystem.
On April 20, Mask Network co-hosted Web3 Social Day at the Hong Kong Web3 Festival, a major event reflecting Asia’s growing leadership in the Web3 space. The meetup brought together founders and builders to chart the future of decentralized social platforms like Lens Protocol and Firefly.
Mask Network founder Suji Yan delivered a keynote at Buidl Asia 2026 on April 17, warning of a "digital civil war" against monopolistic AI giants. Yan argued that decentralized autonomous organizations (DAOs) are critical to building privacy-first digital infrastructure, positioning Lens and Firefly as tools to challenge centralized gatekeepers.
On March 31, Lens Product Lead Kimmo Siren revealed technical advancements at EthCC[9] in Cannes. These included solutions for user experience friction, AI spam mitigation, and cost-effective scalability using Ethereum’s EIP-4844. These innovations aim to shift decentralized social from speculative “SocialFi” projects to sustainable ecosystems.
Web3.bio rolled out wallet-to-wallet messaging powered by XMTP, emphasizing secure and direct communication as an essential layer for decentralized identity and interactions.
The month also brought attention to how narratives affect market dynamics. Notable incidents included:
Mask Network’s focus on decentralized social and infrastructure reflects a broader trend in Web3: the shift from speculative hype to building sustainable, user-focused ecosystems. With AI integration becoming a key theme, the lines between social, identity, and messaging are blurring, paving the way for more robust digital sovereignty tools.
As the Hong Kong Web3 Festival and EthCC[9] demonstrated, global interest in Web3 continues to grow. However, security incidents like Hyperbridge’s exploitation serve as a sobering reminder that the industry must balance innovation with vigilance.
Looking ahead, Mask Network’s progress with Lens, Firefly, and Web3.bio positions it as a critical player in shaping the next phase of the internet. Whether it’s through trading leagues or messaging platforms, the push toward decentralized infrastructure is gaining momentum, even as the market moves past April’s narratives.

item.title: Kraken Pushes for Crypto Tax Reforms After Issuing 56M Forms in 2025 | Kraken calls for US crypto tax reforms, including a de minimis exemption and changes to s... https://Blockchain.News/news/kraken-calls-for-crypto-tax-reforms
item.description:
Original title: Kraken Pushes for Crypto Tax Reforms After Issuing 56M Forms in 2025 Summary: Kraken calls for US crypto tax reforms, including a de minimis exemption and changes to staking income rules, after issuing 56M tax forms in 2025. Article text: Kraken calls for US crypto tax reforms, including a de minimis exemption and changes to staking income rules, after issuing 56M tax forms in 2025.
Kraken, one of the largest cryptocurrency exchanges in the United States, has called for significant tax policy reforms after issuing an eye-popping 56 million tax forms for the 2025 tax year. In a blog post on April 22, the company advocated for a de minimis exemption on small crypto transactions and changes to the treatment of staking income, arguing these moves would reduce bureaucratic overload and ease compliance burdens for millions of taxpayers.
The exchange revealed that nearly 18.5 million of the tax forms it issued were tied to transactions valued at less than $1, and 75% of all forms reported amounts under $50. Kraken characterized the current reporting requirements as “millions of unnecessary forms” generated by trivial transactions. Under current IRS rules, every taxable event—no matter how small—must be reported, creating administrative headaches for both exchanges and users.
Kraken's centerpiece recommendation is a de minimis exemption that would exclude small, routine crypto transactions from capital gains reporting. Such exemptions are not unprecedented; for example, foreign currency transactions under $200 are already exempt from similar reporting requirements under U.S. tax law. The exchange also proposed ending the taxation of so-called “phantom income” from staking rewards, which requires taxpayers to report and pay taxes on cryptocurrency rewards they have not yet sold or converted.
“This is not about helping crypto companies,” Kraken emphasized in its statement. “It’s about simplifying life for 55 million Americans who are using a tax system designed before digital assets existed.”
Staking rewards have been a regulatory gray area in U.S. tax policy. According to IRS guidelines, staking income is taxable at the time a user gains “dominion and control” over the rewards, meaning when they are free to use or transfer the assets. Taxpayers are required to report the fair market value of the rewards as income, and any future sale or transfer triggers capital gains taxes. This double taxation framework has been criticized for being overly burdensome and out of sync with the nature of staking.
The IRS clarified its stance on staking income in Revenue Ruling 2023-14, confirming that rewards are taxable as ordinary income upon receipt. Kraken’s push for reform could resonate with taxpayers frustrated by the complexities and financial implications of these rules, especially as staking becomes more popular.
While some lawmakers have floated the idea of a de minimis exemption for crypto transactions, recent legislative drafts have been narrow in scope. The most recent proposal in Congress, for example, suggests exempting only stablecoin transactions under $200 from IRS reporting requirements—leaving Bitcoin (BTC) and other cryptocurrencies out of the equation entirely.
Tax compliance costs are also a growing concern. A March 2026 report from the Tax Foundation estimated that U.S. taxpayers spend $146 billion annually on tax preparation, including time and out-of-pocket expenses. Meanwhile, the IRS’s decision to end its free Direct File program in late 2025 has only added to the financial strain.
Beyond tax reform, Kraken’s leadership has signaled that the company is still considering an initial public offering (IPO). After filing confidentially with the SEC in November 2025, Kraken co-CEO Arjun Sethi recently indicated that the exchange could go public soon, despite challenging market conditions.
The push for tax reform, combined with an IPO on the horizon, underscores Kraken’s broader strategy to position itself as a leader in both crypto innovation and regulatory dialogue. As U.S. lawmakers and regulators continue to grapple with the complexities of digital assets, Kraken’s proposals could influence how the industry and its users are taxed in the years to come.
For now, all eyes will be on Congress to see whether these reforms gain traction—and whether the crypto industry can finally see some relief from its onerous tax obligations.

item.title: Aave Deposits Plunge $15B After Kelp DAO Exploit | A $293M exploit on Kelp DAO has triggered a $15B liquidity outflow from Aave, exposing vu... https://Blockchain.News/news/aave-deposits-drop-15b-kelp-dao-exploit
item.description:
Original title: Aave Deposits Plunge $15B After Kelp DAO Exploit Summary: A $293M exploit on Kelp DAO has triggered a $15B liquidity outflow from Aave, exposing vulnerabilities in DeFi lending protocols. Article text: A $293M exploit on Kelp DAO has triggered a $15B liquidity outflow from Aave, exposing vulnerabilities in DeFi lending protocols.
Aave, the leading decentralized lending protocol, has seen its deposit base shrink by $15 billion following a $293 million exploit on Kelp DAO. According to Aavescan data, the total value supplied to Aave plummeted from $45.8 billion on Saturday to $30.8 billion as of Wednesday.
The exploit targeted Kelp DAO’s LayerZero-powered rsETH bridge, draining 116,500 restaked Ether (rsETH). The attacker reportedly used some of the stolen funds to borrow from Aave, creating a ripple effect on the platform's liquidity. Aave’s incident report revealed that 89,567 rsETH were deposited into the protocol, leaving a shortfall projected between $123 million and $230 million, depending on how losses are allocated.
Market participants are now concerned about a broader liquidity crunch in decentralized finance (DeFi), with the exploit exacerbating fears of systemic risks. Aave's v3 Wrapped Ether (WETH) market hit 100% utilization temporarily, freezing liquidity for withdrawals, according to Talos, an institutional trading platform. This incident has prompted calls for more robust collateral frameworks to mitigate cascading risks in interconnected DeFi ecosystems.
While Aave grapples with the fallout, other protocols are benefiting. SparkLend, the fourth-largest DeFi lending platform, has seen its total value locked (TVL) increase by $1.3 billion since the exploit, suggesting that capital is flowing to perceived safer venues.
Aave has taken steps to stabilize its markets, unfrozen WETH reserves on its Ethereum Core V3 platform, allowing users to deposit WETH once again. However, reserves remain frozen on other networks, including Arbitrum, Base, and Mantle, limiting liquidity options for users.
How Aave addresses its bad debt could have far-reaching implications. The platform’s risk manager has outlined two scenarios. The first would distribute losses across all rsETH holders on Ethereum mainnet and layer-2s, leaving $123 million in bad debt. The second scenario shifts the burden entirely to layer-2 networks, increasing the shortfall to $230 million.
Prediction markets, such as Polymarket, are already seeing activity. Currently, 20% of traders are betting that Kelp DAO will socialize the losses across all rsETH holders, rather than isolating the impact on layer-2 users. The final decision could set a precedent for how DeFi platforms manage liability in future exploits.
This incident underscores the vulnerabilities in DeFi’s layered ecosystems, where exploits can cascade across platforms, amplifying their impact. For now, traders and investors are closely watching Aave’s next moves, as well as the broader implications for DeFi's resilience.

Aave, the leading decentralized lending protocol, has seen its deposit base shrink by $15 billion following a $293 million exploit on Kelp DAO. According to Aavescan data, the total value supplied to Aave plummeted from $45.8 billion on Saturday to $30.8 billion as of Wednesday.
The exploit targeted Kelp DAO’s LayerZero-powered rsETH bridge, draining 116,500 restaked Ether (rsETH). The attacker reportedly used some of the stolen funds to borrow from Aave, creating a ripple effect on the platform's liquidity. Aave’s incident report revealed that 89,567 rsETH were deposited into the protocol, leaving a shortfall projected between $123 million and $230 million, depending on how losses are allocated.
Market participants are now concerned about a broader liquidity crunch in decentralized finance (DeFi), with the exploit exacerbating fears of systemic risks. Aave's v3 Wrapped Ether (WETH) market hit 100% utilization temporarily, freezing liquidity for withdrawals, according to Talos, an institutional trading platform. This incident has prompted calls for more robust collateral frameworks to mitigate cascading risks in interconnected DeFi ecosystems.
While Aave grapples with the fallout, other protocols are benefiting. SparkLend, the fourth-largest DeFi lending platform, has seen its total value locked (TVL) increase by $1.3 billion since the exploit, suggesting that capital is flowing to perceived safer venues.
Aave has taken steps to stabilize its markets, unfrozen WETH reserves on its Ethereum Core V3 platform, allowing users to deposit WETH once again. However, reserves remain frozen on other networks, including Arbitrum, Base, and Mantle, limiting liquidity options for users.
How Aave addresses its bad debt could have far-reaching implications. The platform’s risk manager has outlined two scenarios. The first would distribute losses across all rsETH holders on Ethereum mainnet and layer-2s, leaving $123 million in bad debt. The second scenario shifts the burden entirely to layer-2 networks, increasing the shortfall to $230 million.
Prediction markets, such as Polymarket, are already seeing activity. Currently, 20% of traders are betting that Kelp DAO will socialize the losses across all rsETH holders, rather than isolating the impact on layer-2 users. The final decision could set a precedent for how DeFi platforms manage liability in future exploits.
This incident underscores the vulnerabilities in DeFi’s layered ecosystems, where exploits can cascade across platforms, amplifying their impact. For now, traders and investors are closely watching Aave’s next moves, as well as the broader implications for DeFi's resilience.

item.title: CRV Breakdown Imminent: $0.20 Target Within 10 Days | Curve's rally to $0.24 masks serious structural weakness as selling volume dominates and... https://Blockchain.News/news/20260422-crv-breakdown-imminent-020-target-within-10-days-prediction
item.description:
Original title: CRV Breakdown Imminent: $0.20 Target Within 10 Days Summary: Curve's rally to $0.24 masks serious structural weakness as selling volume dominates and key resistance holds firm. Price breaks $0.22 support next week with $0.20 becoming the primary downside tar Article text: Curve's rally to $0.24 masks serious structural weakness as selling volume dominates and key resistance holds firm. Price breaks $0.22 support next week with $0.20 becoming the primary downside tar...
CRV's 2.43% bounce over 24 hours has stalled exactly where bears wanted it - right at the upper Bollinger Band resistance near $0.24. This isn't coincidence. The price action screams distribution as smart money uses retail buying to unload positions.
The technical picture has deteriorated sharply. MACD histogram sits flat at zero while price trades 90% up the Bollinger channel - a combination that historically marks local tops. RSI at 56.53 shows momentum already fading despite the recent bounce. More damaging is the volume profile: $3.7M in spot volume with taker sell orders outpacing buys by nearly 20%.
The 20-day SMA at $0.22 represents CRV's last meaningful support before a more serious decline unfolds. Short-term moving averages are converging around $0.23, creating a compression that typically resolves with violent moves. Given the bearish volume and momentum divergence, this breaks lower.
Below $0.22, there's minimal support until the lower Bollinger Band around $0.20. The 200-day SMA sits way up at $0.36, highlighting just how damaged CRV's long-term structure has become. This isn't a healthy pullback in an uptrend - this is a bear market bounce running out of steam.
Futures positioning reveals a dangerous setup for bulls. Whales hold 64.6% long positions while retail maintains 60.1% long exposure. When both groups crowd the same side while price fails to break resistance, liquidation cascades become inevitable.
The 0.0100% funding rate staying neutral despite bullish positioning suggests the market hasn't priced in coming volatility. Open interest declining 0.47% confirms smart money is reducing exposure ahead of the next major move. These conditions typically precede sharp selloffs.
CRV faces a binary outcome over the next 5-10 days. Either price breaks cleanly above $0.245 on genuine buying volume above $5M daily, or the current resistance holds and triggers the breakdown sequence.
The breakdown scenario carries higher probability based on current technicals. Any rejection from current levels targets $0.22 first, then $0.20 as the primary objective. The risk-reward strongly favors shorts here - stops above $0.25 limit downside to 4% while targets offer 8-16% gains.
Volume and momentum indicators align for lower prices. CRV's structural decline from higher levels continues, and this bounce appears to be giving trapped longs a final exit opportunity before the next leg down accelerates.
Hourly candlesticks (about 96 bars), same endpoint as our cryptocurrency price pages. Numbers below refresh from 1-minute klines.
The market has spoken through price action and volume - CRV breaks $0.22 support within the next week, making $0.20 the logical target for aggressive sellers.

item.title: Lazarus Group Deploys macOS Malware Targeting Crypto Execs | Lazarus Group launches macOS malware targeting crypto and fintech executives via fake Zoo... https://Blockchain.News/news/lazarus-group-macos-malware-crypto-executives
item.description:
Original title: Lazarus Group Deploys macOS Malware Targeting Crypto Execs Summary: Lazarus Group launches macOS malware targeting crypto and fintech executives via fake Zoom calls, aiming to compromise critical credentials. Article text: Lazarus Group launches macOS malware targeting crypto and fintech executives via fake Zoom calls, aiming to compromise critical credentials.
The notorious Lazarus Group, linked to the North Korean government, has launched a new macOS malware campaign targeting cryptocurrency and fintech executives. Dubbed "Mach-O Man," the malware is distributed via fake Zoom or Google Meet calls, where victims unknowingly execute commands that install the malware in the background.
According to a report by Mauro Eldritch, founder of threat intelligence firm BCA Ltd., the malware exploits social engineering techniques to bypass traditional security controls. Once installed, it extracts sensitive information such as browser credentials, cookies, macOS Keychain entries, and corporate access data. The stolen data is then exfiltrated to attackers via Telegram, followed by a self-deletion script to cover its tracks.
This campaign underscores Lazarus Group's continued evolution in targeting not just cryptocurrency-native firms but also traditional businesses involved in fintech. Security researchers warn that the malware could lead to account takeovers, financial losses, and exposure of critical corporate data.
The Lazarus Group, active since at least 2009, is infamous for a series of high-profile cyberattacks. It has been linked to the $81 million Bangladesh Bank heist in 2016, the global WannaCry ransomware attack in 2017, and the $620 million Ronin Network hack in 2022. More recently, in 2025, the group reportedly orchestrated the $1.4 billion Bybit hack—the largest cryptocurrency theft to date.
The group's activities are believed to fund North Korea's state programs, including weapons development, while circumventing international sanctions. Over the years, Lazarus has demonstrated a knack for adapting its tactics, shifting from targeting financial institutions to cryptocurrency platforms, and now, macOS users.
This latest attack highlights the increasing risks posed by state-sponsored hacking groups to the crypto and fintech sectors. With billions of dollars in digital assets at stake, cybersecurity remains a critical concern for companies operating in these industries. The Lazarus Group’s pivot to macOS indicates a widening scope of attacks, likely in response to enhanced security measures on other platforms.
For traders and businesses, the implications are clear: heightened vigilance is necessary. Organizations should consider implementing robust endpoint security, employee training on phishing schemes, and network segmentation to limit the fallout of potential breaches.
As Lazarus Group expands its toolkit, it serves as a reminder that the cryptocurrency space remains a prime target for sophisticated cybercriminals. The group's history of adapting to new technologies and platforms emphasizes the need for proactive security measures. With the global crypto market cap exceeding $1 trillion as of 2026, the stakes for securing digital assets have never been higher.
Businesses and executives in the crypto and fintech sectors must stay informed about emerging threats and invest in advanced threat detection and response capabilities. The Lazarus Group’s latest campaign is a stark warning: no platform or operating system is immune.

item.title: MiCA Deadline Looms: Small Crypto Firms Face EU Compliance Crunch | EU's MiCA regulation demands compliance by July 1, 2026, forcing smaller crypto firms to... https://Blockchain.News/news/mica-deadline-small-crypto-firms
item.description:
Original title: MiCA Deadline Looms: Small Crypto Firms Face EU Compliance Crunch Summary: EU's MiCA regulation demands compliance by July 1, 2026, forcing smaller crypto firms to adapt or exit as costs and consolidation rise. Article text: EU's MiCA regulation demands compliance by July 1, 2026, forcing smaller crypto firms to adapt or exit as costs and consolidation rise.
The clock is ticking for crypto firms in the European Union as the transitional period for the Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) regulation ends on July 1, 2026. Smaller players are under mounting pressure to secure compliance or cease operations within the bloc. MiCA’s sweeping rules, designed to harmonize crypto-asset oversight across the EU, are raising compliance costs and could trigger market consolidation, particularly among lightly capitalized firms.
The regulation, which came into force in June 2023, requires crypto-asset service providers (CASPs) to obtain licenses to continue serving EU clients. While larger, well-established firms like CoinJar have already secured licenses—viewing MiCA as a necessary framework for legitimacy—many smaller operators are struggling to meet the authorization, governance, and reporting requirements.
In Poland, where approximately 2,000 virtual asset service providers (VASPs) operate, only one—Ari10—has successfully obtained a MiCA license, according to its founder, Mateusz Kara. Speaking to Cointelegraph, Kara warned that compliance costs and organizational requirements leave little room for smaller players, potentially forcing many to shutter. This echoes patterns seen in Japan, where post-2018 licensing reforms drove smaller exchanges out of the market.
“The market is heading for consolidation,” said Matthew Pinnock, COO of the decentralized finance platform Altura. He added that larger, well-capitalized exchanges and custodians are better positioned to weather MiCA’s regulatory demands, leaving smaller or hybrid projects at a disadvantage.
Despite MiCA’s exemption for fully decentralized services under Recital 22, industry insiders argue the definition remains too vague. Many decentralized finance (DeFi) protocols, which rely on hybrid structures like upgradeable smart contracts or centralized front ends, could fall into a regulatory gray zone.
Altura’s Pinnock noted that while the platform operates non-custodial strategies, certain features might still attract regulatory scrutiny. Taran Dhillon, head of digital assets at the decentralized impact investment platform Kula, described the exemption as insufficiently clear, leaving developers in “regulatory limbo.” This prolonged uncertainty could drive innovative projects offshore, undermining the EU’s goal of becoming a global crypto hub.
EU regulators maintain that MiCA balances innovation with investor protection. The European Securities and Markets Authority (ESMA) emphasized that the framework was designed to be proportionate to the risks posed by different firms. Smaller players are not expected to meet the same requirements as systemically important entities, according to ESMA. However, the agency supports centralizing supervision for major cross-border exchanges, arguing it would streamline oversight and reduce regulatory arbitrage.
Not all agree. Malta’s Financial Services Authority (MFSA) warned that a one-size-fits-all approach might overlook local market nuances, especially given MiCA’s relatively recent implementation.
While MiCA represents a compliance hurdle for smaller firms, early adopters like CoinJar’s CEO, Asher Tan, see it as a filter that will ultimately strengthen the market. “MiCA brings crypto in line with serious financial frameworks,” Tan said, adding that the regulation encourages long-term value creation over speculative activity.
For firms that can adapt, MiCA offers a clear, passportable path to scale across the EU. However, as the July 1 deadline approaches, the industry faces a critical test: whether MiCA will cement Europe as a trusted crypto hub or push the next wave of innovation elsewhere remains to be seen.
EU's MiCA regulation demands compliance by July 1, 2026, forcing smaller crypto firms to adapt or exit as costs and consolidation rise.

Smaller players are not expected to meet the same requirements as systemically important entities, according to ESMA. However, the agency supports centralizing supervision for major cross-border exchanges, arguing it would streamline oversight and reduce regulatory arbitrage.
Not all agree. Malta’s Financial Services Authority (MFSA) warned that a one-size-fits-all approach might overlook local market nuances, especially given MiCA’s relatively recent implementation. Source: https://Blockchain.News/news/mica-deadline-small-crypto-firms
item.content:encoded:
EU's MiCA regulation demands compliance by July 1, 2026, forcing smaller crypto firms to adapt or exit as costs and consolidation rise.
The clock is ticking for crypto firms in the European Union as the transitional period for the Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) regulation ends on July 1, 2026. Smaller players are under mounting pressure to secure compliance or cease operations within the bloc. MiCA’s sweeping rules, designed to harmonize crypto-asset oversight across the EU, are raising compliance costs and could trigger market consolidation, particularly among lightly capitalized firms.
The regulation, which came into force in June 2023, requires crypto-asset service providers (CASPs) to obtain licenses to continue serving EU clients. While larger, well-established firms like CoinJar have already secured licenses—viewing MiCA as a necessary framework for legitimacy—many smaller operators are struggling to meet the authorization, governance, and reporting requirements.
In Poland, where approximately 2,000 virtual asset service providers (VASPs) operate, only one—Ari10—has successfully obtained a MiCA license, according to its founder, Mateusz Kara. Speaking to Cointelegraph, Kara warned that compliance costs and organizational requirements leave little room for smaller players, potentially forcing many to shutter. This echoes patterns seen in Japan, where post-2018 licensing reforms drove smaller exchanges out of the market.
“The market is heading for consolidation,” said Matthew Pinnock, COO of the decentralized finance platform Altura. He added that larger, well-capitalized exchanges and custodians are better positioned to weather MiCA’s regulatory demands, leaving smaller or hybrid projects at a disadvantage.
Despite MiCA’s exemption for fully decentralized services under Recital 22, industry insiders argue the definition remains too vague. Many decentralized finance (DeFi) protocols, which rely on hybrid structures like upgradeable smart contracts or centralized front ends, could fall into a regulatory gray zone.
Altura’s Pinnock noted that while the platform operates non-custodial strategies, certain features might still attract regulatory scrutiny. Taran Dhillon, head of digital assets at the decentralized impact investment platform Kula, described the exemption as insufficiently clear, leaving developers in “regulatory limbo.” This prolonged uncertainty could drive innovative projects offshore, undermining the EU’s goal of becoming a global crypto hub.
EU regulators maintain that MiCA balances innovation with investor protection. The European Securities and Markets Authority (ESMA) emphasized that the framework was designed to be proportionate to the risks posed by different firms. Smaller players are not expected to meet the same requirements as systemically important entities, according to ESMA. However, the agency supports centralizing supervision for major cross-border exchanges, arguing it would streamline oversight and reduce regulatory arbitrage.
Not all agree. Malta’s Financial Services Authority (MFSA) warned that a one-size-fits-all approach might overlook local market nuances, especially given MiCA’s relatively recent implementation.
While MiCA represents a compliance hurdle for smaller firms, early adopters like CoinJar’s CEO, Asher Tan, see it as a filter that will ultimately strengthen the market. “MiCA brings crypto in line with serious financial frameworks,” Tan said, adding that the regulation encourages long-term value creation over speculative activity.
For firms that can adapt, MiCA offers a clear, passportable path to scale across the EU. However, as the July 1 deadline approaches, the industry faces a critical test: whether MiCA will cement Europe as a trusted crypto hub or push the next wave of innovation elsewhere remains to be seen.

item.title: APT Primed for Bull Trap Rally to $1.20 Before $0.75 Crash | Aptos shows classic distribution patterns at $0.97 with derivatives positioning screaming... https://Blockchain.News/news/20260422-prediction-apt-primed-for-bull-trap-rally-to
item.description:
Original title: APT Primed for Bull Trap Rally to $1.20 Before $0.75 Crash Summary: Aptos shows classic distribution patterns at $0.97 with derivatives positioning screaming institutional exit. Expect dead cat bounce to $1.20 resistance followed by violent 35% correction to $0.75 Article text: Aptos shows classic distribution patterns at $0.97 with derivatives positioning screaming institutional exit. Expect dead cat bounce to $1.20 resistance followed by violent 35% correction to $0.75 ...
Aptos painted the perfect bull trap setup today, climbing 3.42% to $0.97 while smart money quietly heads for the exits. The taker buy/sell ratio at 0.72 exposes the underlying weakness - aggressive selling dominates despite the green candle. This divergence between surface price action and actual order flow marks textbook distribution, not the accumulation retail traders are betting on.
The derivatives market tells an even uglier story. Top traders maintain 62.8% long positions while retail sits at 58.6% long, creating a dangerous consensus trade. When positioning becomes this crowded with declining open interest down 0.76%, the stage is set for maximum pain. The neutral funding rate at 0.01% removes any immediate squeeze pressure, giving institutions perfect conditions to time their exit.
APT's current positioning near the upper Bollinger Band at 86% creates the ideal launching pad for false hope. The MACD histogram sits at absolute zero while RSI hovers at 56 - not oversold enough to prevent further upside but perfectly positioned for momentum failure. This combination typically produces sharp rallies that die spectacular deaths.
The moving average structure reveals the bigger picture damage. While APT managed to reclaim the 7-day SMA at $0.96, it trades 47% below the 200-day SMA at $1.83. Short-term averages clustering around $0.90-$0.93 will act as gravitational pull once this bounce exhausts itself. The massive gap between current price and longer-term trend represents pure air pocket territory.
Market structure points toward a calculated two-phase move designed to trap both sides. Phase one targets $1.20 resistance where I expect violent rejection within one week. This level represents the final liquidity grab before institutional selling overwhelms diminishing buyer demand.
The optimal short entry zone sits at $1.15-$1.20 with tight stops at $1.25. Initial resistance appears at $1.01, but the real money comes from positioning early for the second phase crash toward $0.75 strong support. This downside target represents a 35-40% correction that will eliminate every support level between current price and major demand.
Hourly candlesticks (about 96 bars), same endpoint as our cryptocurrency price pages. Numbers below refresh from 1-minute klines.
Invalidation occurs only if APT reclaims and holds above $1.25 for more than 48 hours, suggesting genuine institutional accumulation rather than distribution. Given current derivative positioning and the absence of fundamental catalysts, this scenario remains highly unlikely.
The most probable path sees APT touching $1.20 within two weeks, followed by the kind of correction that reminds everyone why position sizing matters. The setup carries high conviction, but markets can remain irrational far longer than most traders can maintain their margin calls. Trade accordingly.

item.title: Russia Advances Crypto Bill as First Reading Passes State Duma | Russia's parliament moves forward with a crypto bill mandating licensed intermediaries an... https://Blockchain.News/news/russia-crypto-bill-first-reading
item.description:
Original title: Russia Advances Crypto Bill as First Reading Passes State Duma Summary: Russia's parliament moves forward with a crypto bill mandating licensed intermediaries and imposing strict penalties on unregistered platforms. Article text: Russia's parliament moves forward with a crypto bill mandating licensed intermediaries and imposing strict penalties on unregistered platforms.
Russia’s State Duma has approved the first reading of a sweeping crypto regulation bill, setting the stage for significant changes to the country’s digital asset market. The draft legislation, titled “On Digital Currency and Digital Rights” (Bill No. 1194918-8), proposes channeling all cryptocurrency transactions through licensed intermediaries under the supervision of the Bank of Russia. If passed into law, these rules could take effect as early as July 2026, with unlicensed platforms banned outright starting July 2027.
The bill, along with three related proposals, aims to establish a regulated crypto ecosystem while introducing investment caps for retail traders, mandatory regulatory compliance, and strict penalties for violations. Retail investors, for instance, would face a purchase limit of 300,000 rubles (approximately $4,000) annually through any single licensed intermediary. Only “most liquid digital currencies”—those with a market cap exceeding 5 trillion rubles ($66.6 billion) and a five-year trading history—would qualify for trading under the proposed rules.
A related proposal, Bill No. 1209607-8, seeks to impose criminal penalties on operators of unregistered crypto services, including imprisonment and fines. However, the Russian Supreme Court has expressed reservations about this measure, calling it premature given the absence of a fully adopted regulatory framework. Critics argue that pushing for criminal enforcement without clear guidelines could lead to confusion and unintended consequences for the burgeoning sector.
Russia’s approach to cryptocurrency regulation has shifted markedly in recent years. While crypto ownership and trading remain legal, domestic payments using digital assets are prohibited. The Bank of Russia has taken a central role in shaping the country’s digital currency policies, including its rollout of the digital ruble. Recent legislative efforts build on a 2023 framework that formally recognized cryptocurrencies as property, but the new bills aim to tighten oversight further.
These developments are part of a broader strategy to reduce the crypto market’s “grey zone,” align with anti-financial crime initiatives, and mitigate risks to consumers. However, industry participants warn that over-regulation could have the opposite effect, driving crypto activity underground rather than encouraging compliance. The emphasis on licensed intermediaries also raises questions about the future of peer-to-peer trading in Russia.
For international observers, Russia’s crypto policies are particularly significant given the geopolitical backdrop of sanctions. While domestic crypto use remains tightly controlled, digital assets are reportedly being explored as a tool for cross-border transactions and international trade.
The bill must pass additional readings before becoming law, meaning its final provisions could still change. Meanwhile, the Bank of Russia is likely to play a pivotal role in defining which assets qualify as “most liquid” and setting compliance standards.
As global crypto markets watch Russia’s regulatory moves closely, traders should keep an eye on potential enforcement timelines and any shifts in market accessibility. If implemented, these laws could reshape the dynamics of crypto trading across one of the largest markets in Eastern Europe.

item.title: LINK Eyes $11-12 Breakout as Smart Money Loads Up During Price Compression | Chainlink trades near $9.52 while 69% of top traders position long, setting up a potentia... https://Blockchain.News/news/20260422-price-prediction-link-eyes-11-12-breakout-as-smart
item.description:
Original title: LINK Eyes $11-12 Breakout as Smart Money Loads Up During Price Compression Summary: Chainlink trades near $9.52 while 69% of top traders position long, setting up a potential run to $11-12 as the current consolidation pattern approaches its breaking point. Article text: Chainlink trades near $9.52 while 69% of top traders position long, setting up a potential run to $11-12 as the current consolidation pattern approaches its breaking point.
TITLE: LINK Eyes $11-12 Breakout as Smart Money Loads Up During Price Compression
The price action around $9.52 tells a story of deliberate accumulation rather than indecision. While LINK grinds sideways between key levels, the underlying mechanics suggest this consolidation is building pressure for the next major move higher.
The RSI holding steady at 56.56 reflects a market in equilibrium - neither sellers dominating with oversold readings nor buyers getting carried away into overbought territory. This neutral momentum reading during sideways price action often precedes directional breakouts when combined with other confirming factors.
More telling is how price continues to test the $9.64-$9.76 resistance zone without getting firmly rejected. Each test that fails to produce significant selling pressure weakens that ceiling and increases the likelihood of an eventual breakthrough.
The derivatives market reveals where sophisticated traders are placing their bets. Top traders maintain a 2.23:1 long-to-short ratio with 69% positioned bullish - a stark contrast to typical retail behavior during sideways markets. These aren't momentum chasers; they're positioned ahead of the anticipated move.
Daily spot volume on Binance alone hits $24.2 million, indicating sustained institutional interest despite the lack of dramatic price movement. The $82 million in open interest holding steady through recent minor declines shows leveraged positions aren't getting forced out by normal volatility.
The balanced taker buy/sell ratio at 0.945 confirms neither panic selling nor euphoric buying is driving current action. Instead, it points to methodical accumulation by patient capital willing to build positions during this quiet phase.
The compression between $8.40 and $9.60 has lasted long enough to create meaningful technical significance. When volatility contracts this much - reflected in the daily ATR of $0.42 - markets typically respond with sharp directional moves once a catalyst emerges.
Hourly candlesticks (about 96 bars), same endpoint as our cryptocurrency price pages. Numbers below refresh from 1-minute klines.
A decisive break above $9.76 would likely trigger algorithmic buying programs and force covering from the minority short positions. This mechanical response could accelerate price movement toward the $10 psychological level, which then becomes the gateway to higher targets.
The MACD sitting near zero while price holds in the upper portion of its recent range suggests momentum is coiled rather than dissipating. This combination often precedes explosive moves when the technical setup finally resolves.
The path forward depends on clearing that $9.76 resistance with conviction. A breakout above $10 opens the door to the $11-12 zone within the next month, supported by the current smart money positioning and compressed volatility environment.
Should the bullish scenario unfold, the initial target sits around $11.50 based on the width of the current consolidation pattern. A secondary target near $12 becomes viable if momentum sustains and retail interest follows institutional positioning.
The alternative scenario requires a breakdown below $9.12 support, which would invalidate the accumulation thesis and likely target the $8.40 area. However, current positioning data and volume characteristics suggest this remains the less probable outcome given how smart money is positioned.
Risk/reward clearly favors the upside here. The combination of patient institutional accumulation, compressed volatility, and a well-defined technical setup creates conditions that historically resolve in favor of the direction where sophisticated money is positioned.

Chainlink trades near $9.52 while 69% of top traders position long, setting up a potential run to $11-12 as the current consolidation pattern approaches its breaking point.
TITLE: LINK Eyes $11-12 Breakout as Smart Money Loads Up During Price Compression
The price action around $9.52 tells a story of deliberate accumulation rather than indecision. While LINK grinds sideways between key levels, the underlying mechanics suggest this consolidation is building pressure for the next major move higher.
The RSI holding steady at 56.56 reflects a market in equilibrium - neither sellers dominating with oversold readings nor buyers getting carried away into overbought territory. This neutral momentum reading during sideways price action often precedes directional breakouts when combined with other confirming factors.
More telling is how price continues to test the $9.64-$9.76 resistance zone without getting firmly rejected. Each test that fails to produce significant selling pressure weakens that ceiling and increases the likelihood of an eventual breakthrough.
The derivatives market reveals where sophisticated traders are placing their bets. Top traders maintain a 2.23:1 long-to-short ratio with 69% positioned bullish - a stark contrast to typical retail behavior during sideways markets. These aren't momentum chasers; they're positioned ahead of the anticipated move.
Daily spot volume on Binance alone hits $24.2 million, indicating sustained institutional interest despite the lack of dramatic price movement. The $82 million in open interest holding steady through recent minor declines shows leveraged positions aren't getting forced out by normal volatility.
The balanced taker buy/sell ratio at 0.945 confirms neither panic selling nor euphoric buying is driving current action. Instead, it points to methodical accumulation by patient capital willing to build positions during this quiet phase.
The compression between $8.40 and $9.60 has lasted long enough to create meaningful technical significance. When volatility contracts this much - reflected in the daily ATR of $0.42 - markets typically respond with sharp directional moves once a catalyst emerges.
Hourly candlesticks (about 96 bars), same endpoint as our cryptocurrency price pages. Numbers below refresh from 1-minute klines.

item.title: AVAX Tests $11 Breakout as Whale Activity Surges Above Key Support | Avalanche consolidates near $9.50 with technical indicators suggesting a push toward $11... https://Blockchain.News/news/20260422-prediction-avax-tests-11-breakout-as-whale-activity
item.description:
Original title: AVAX Tests $11 Breakout as Whale Activity Surges Above Key Support Summary: Avalanche consolidates near $9.50 with technical indicators suggesting a push toward $11 within two weeks. Strong support at $9.23 and neutral momentum readings create favorable conditions for upsi Article text: Avalanche consolidates near $9.50 with technical indicators suggesting a push toward $11 within two weeks. Strong support at $9.23 and neutral momentum readings create favorable conditions for upsi...
Avalanche trades at $9.51 within a tight consolidation range, bounded by resistance at $9.74 and support at $9.23. The RSI reading of 53.17 sits comfortably in neutral territory, providing room for upward movement without immediately triggering overbought conditions. Meanwhile, the flatlining MACD histogram indicates momentum remains compressed, typical of assets preparing for directional moves.
The Bollinger Band positioning shows AVAX at 0.71 of the range between bands, suggesting underlying strength despite recent sideways action. Price continues holding above the 20-day SMA at $9.29, which has served as a reliable support level throughout recent trading sessions.
Daily trading volume of $28.1 million reflects steady institutional interest without excessive speculative activity. The derivatives positioning shows institutional traders maintaining a bullish bias, though funding rates remain near neutral at 0.01%, indicating balanced leverage across long and short positions.
Open interest has increased by 0.16%, suggesting new capital entering positions rather than existing traders simply rotating. This fresh positioning often precedes significant price movements as market participants establish stakes ahead of anticipated volatility.
The convergence of neutral RSI positioning and compressed MACD creates conditions favorable for upward momentum. A break above $9.74 resistance would likely target the $10.50 area initially, with extension potential toward $11.50 if buying pressure sustains.
The technical setup resembles classic accumulation patterns where controlled volatility gives way to trending moves. AVAX's ability to maintain current levels despite broader market uncertainty demonstrates relative strength that could translate into outperformance.
Below current support at $9.23, the next significant level sits at $8.94. A breakdown through these areas would signal weakness and potentially trigger further selling pressure from leveraged positions.
The key resistance at $9.74 has held multiple tests recently, making a decisive break above this level crucial for confirming bullish momentum. Without this breakout, AVAX may remain range-bound between current support and resistance levels.
Technical indicators align for potential upside movement toward $11 over the next 14 days, assuming current support levels hold. The $10.50 area represents the initial target following any breakout above $9.74 resistance.
Traders should monitor the $9.23 support level closely, as sustained trading below this area would invalidate near-term bullish expectations and suggest further downside toward $8.94.
Hourly candlesticks (about 96 bars), same endpoint as our cryptocurrency price pages. Numbers below refresh from 1-minute klines.

item.title: NVIDIA and Google Cloud Push Boundaries of AI with Rubin GPUs | NVIDIA's Vera Rubin GPUs and Google Cloud's AI infrastructure pave the way for agentic an... https://Blockchain.News/news/nvidia-google-cloud-rubin-ai-factories
item.description:
Original title: NVIDIA and Google Cloud Push Boundaries of AI with Rubin GPUs Summary: NVIDIA's Vera Rubin GPUs and Google Cloud's AI infrastructure pave the way for agentic and physical AI at unprecedented scales. Article text: NVIDIA's Vera Rubin GPUs and Google Cloud's AI infrastructure pave the way for agentic and physical AI at unprecedented scales.
NVIDIA (NASDAQ: NVDA) and Google Cloud have unveiled a groundbreaking expansion of their decade-long partnership, targeting the next generation of AI applications. Announced at Google Cloud Next 2026 in Las Vegas, the collaboration revolves around NVIDIA’s Vera Rubin-powered A5X instances and Google Cloud’s AI infrastructure, enabling the creation of "AI factories" capable of scaling to nearly 1 million Rubin GPUs.
These AI factories aim to advance agentic and physical AI—systems designed to reason, plan, and act autonomously—while cutting costs and energy use. NVIDIA’s Rubin GPU platform, part of the cutting-edge Blackwell architecture, offers up to four times the inference throughput of its predecessor, the H100, and consumes 25 times less energy for trillion-parameter models. This makes it a crucial leap forward in reducing the cost and environmental impact of AI workloads.
The A5X instances, powered by NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72 rack-scale systems, promise a 10x reduction in inference cost per token and a 10x increase in token throughput per megawatt compared to earlier models. These instances can scale up to 80,000 Rubin GPUs in a single-site cluster and 960,000 GPUs across multisite clusters, offering unparalleled performance for enterprise-scale AI tasks such as training large language models and running real-time inference workloads.
"The next decade of AI will be shaped by the ability to run demanding workloads on integrated, AI-optimized infrastructure," said Mark Lohmeyer, VP and GM of AI and computing infrastructure at Google Cloud. By combining Google Cloud's scalable infrastructure with NVIDIA's Blackwell-based systems, the platform gives enterprises a flexible and cost-effective solution for complex AI workflows.
Beyond infrastructure, the partnership introduces enhancements to NVIDIA’s Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform. Developers can leverage NVIDIA Nemotron and NeMo frameworks to build agentic AI systems capable of managing workflows, simulating environments, and powering robotics. For example, NVIDIA's open models and APIs are now optimized to run in Google Cloud environments, enabling faster training and deployment of agentic AI.
Applications range from cybersecurity tools by CrowdStrike, which fine-tune models for domain-specific threat detection, to industrial solutions like NVIDIA Omniverse and Isaac Sim, which are used to create digital twins and robotics simulations. These tools streamline the transition from design to real-world deployment, spanning industries such as automotive, aerospace, and heavy machinery.
NVIDIA’s Blackwell GPUs bring significant advancements in memory bandwidth, energy efficiency, and tensor performance. These features are crucial for training enormous models and running real-time AI inference. With cloud rental rates for Blackwell GPUs ranging from $2.65/hr to $14.24/hr and complete DGX B200 systems priced at over $500,000, NVIDIA is solidifying its position as the AI hardware leader.
This dominance is reflected in NVIDIA’s current market capitalization of $4.858 trillion, making it the world’s most valuable company. The Vera Rubin platform, with its combination of Rubin GPUs and Vera CPUs, is expected to generate substantial growth opportunities as cloud providers like AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft deploy Rubin-based instances throughout 2026.
This latest collaboration underscores NVIDIA's and Google Cloud's commitment to accelerating the deployment of AI at massive scales. With agentic and physical AI poised to transform industries, enterprises now have access to the tools and infrastructure needed to move from prototyping to production seamlessly. As more developers join the ecosystem—over 90,000 in the past year alone—the potential for innovation is enormous.
For traders and investors, the AI hardware race is a key area to watch. NVIDIA’s ability to maintain its technological lead with products like the Rubin and Blackwell platforms strengthens its growth outlook, while its partnership with Google Cloud adds another layer of defensibility against competitors.
The companies' joint efforts will be showcased in upcoming workshops and demos at Google Cloud Next. With enterprise adoption accelerating, NVIDIA continues to set the standard for the AI era.
